properties.name: Enbridge Mainline
properties.commodity: Crude oil — light, heavy, and diluted bitumen (WCS, synthetic crude)

to.name: Superior, WI / Sarnia, ON / US Gulf Coast

keyFacts: The largest crude oil transportation system in North America by capacity.
Multiple parallel lines (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6A, 6B, 9, 10) carry different grades to refineries in the US Midwest, eastern Canada, and the Gulf Coast.
Line 5 crosses the Straits of Mackinac in Michigan — politically contested for decades over environmental risk.
Line 3 was replaced in 2019–2021 — Enbridge's largest project ever (~$10B CAD).
Originally built in 1950 as the Interprovincial Pipe Line; rebranded as Enbridge in 1998.
properties.notes: On the map: only the western Canadian trunk (Edmonton → Hardisty → through the prairies to the Manitoba / US border) is rendered. The system continues into the US Lakehead network, then re-enters Canada at Sarnia, ON.

properties.name: Trans Mountain Pipeline System
properties.commodity: Crude oil — light crude and diluted bitumen; some refined products
keyFacts: The only Canadian pipeline carrying crude to a Pacific tidewater port — strategic Asian-market access.
TMX (Trans Mountain Expansion) entered service in June 2024, lifting capacity from ~300,000 to 890,000 bbl/day.
Owned by the Government of Canada since 2018, after Kinder Morgan exited amid permitting risk.
Crosses the Rockies via the Yellowhead corridor (Edmonton → Jasper → Kamloops → Hope → Burnaby).
TMX commissioning has narrowed the WCS / WTI price differential as Canadian crude reaches new buyers.
properties.notes: The original Trans Mountain Pipeline (TMP) entered service in 1953. The expansion (TMX) twins most of the corridor and tripled effective egress capacity in 2024.

properties.name: Keystone Pipeline (Phases 1–2)
properties.operator: South Bow Corporation (spun off from TC Energy, October 2024)
properties.commodity: Crude oil — primarily diluted bitumen (WCS) and light synthetic crude
keyFacts: Connects Alberta's oil sands to US refining hubs at Cushing, OK ('the pipeline crossroads of the world') and the Texas Gulf Coast.
Border crossing at Haskett, MB / Walhalla, ND.
Distinct from the cancelled Keystone XL (2021) — the proposed shorter, larger-diameter extension across Montana and South Dakota.
TC Energy spun off its liquids business as 'South Bow' in October 2024 — Keystone is now a South Bow asset.
A December 2022 spill in Kansas (~14,000 bbl) led to capacity restrictions while remediation was underway.
properties.notes: On the map: only the Canadian segment (Hardisty, AB → border crossing at Haskett, MB) is rendered. The system continues through North Dakota, South Dakota, Nebraska, then south to Cushing, OK and the Texas Gulf Coast.

properties.name: Coastal GasLink
properties.operator: Coastal GasLink Pipeline LP (TC Energy + KKR + AIMCo + Indigenous partners)
keyFacts: Feeds LNG Canada at Kitimat — Canada's first major Pacific LNG export project, with first cargoes expected mid-2025.
Carries Montney basin gas (Canada's most prolific gas play) ~670 km west across the BC interior.
Construction faced sustained Wet'suwet'en hereditary chief opposition over consent and unceded territory (2019–2024).
Cost rose from ~$6.6B CAD (2018 estimate) to ~$14.5B CAD at completion in 2023.
Indigenous-led groups acquired a meaningful equity stake in 2024; Cedar LNG is positioned as a future second customer.
properties.notes: First Canadian pipeline built specifically to feed Pacific-coast LNG export. Marks the start of Canada's commercial LNG era after decades of debate and stalled projects.

properties.name: NGTL System
properties.commodity: Natural gas (Montney, Duvernay, Deep Basin, oil-sands gas)
keyFacts: The largest natural gas gathering and transmission system in Western Canada — moves gas from Montney, Duvernay, and Deep Basin producers to market.
Owned by TC Energy under the NOVA Gas Transmission subsidiary; ~24,500 km of pipe with thousands of receipt and delivery points.
Empress, AB is the eastern delivery point — gas hands off to the TC Energy Canadian Mainline for transport east.
AECO/NIT (the trading hub at Empress) is one of the most-quoted natural gas benchmarks in North America.
The Foothills section connects to Northern Border Pipeline at the US border, carrying Western Canadian gas to the US Midwest.
properties.notes: On the map: 4 representative arms shown (Montney gathering, Empress trunk, Foothills/southern leg, Athabasca arm). The actual NGTL system is a network covering most of Alberta and northeast BC.

properties.name: Canadian Mainline (TC Energy)
properties.commodity: Natural gas (WCSB-sourced; some receipts from US Marcellus via Niagara)
keyFacts: Carries Western Canadian gas (handed off from NGTL at Empress) across the country to Ontario, Quebec, and the Maritimes.
~14,000 km of pipe across the Canadian Shield, the Great Lakes basin, and the St. Lawrence corridor.
Has become structurally underutilized as US shale (Marcellus) flipped Eastern Canada from net importer-from-the-west to net importer-from-the-south.
Dawn, Ontario is one of the most liquid North American gas trading hubs outside Henry Hub.
Mainline tolling structure is a perennial regulatory battle — biggest single lever on prairie gas producer netbacks.
properties.notes: Originally built by TransCanada in the 1950s, the Canadian Mainline became a defining piece of Canadian energy infrastructure. The politics and economics around it have shifted more than the steel itself.

properties.name: Westcoast Energy (BC Pipeline)
keyFacts: BC's main gas transmission backbone — moves Montney and Horn River gas south through the BC interior to the US Pacific Northwest.
Sumas / Huntingdon border crossing is a key delivery point to Washington and Oregon gas markets.
Spectra-owned for decades; Enbridge acquired Spectra in 2017, putting it under the same parent as the Mainline crude system.
A 2018 rupture near Prince George reduced capacity for months and exposed corrosion vulnerabilities in the older pipe.
T-South line runs the southern portion; T-North gathers from the BC interior north of Prince George.
properties.notes: BC's primary pipeline export route for gas heading to US Pacific Northwest markets.
